evals.prompt: How do I configure ESLint for a strict TypeScript project?
evals.expected_output: Use @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin with typescript-eslint/recommended-type-checked. Enable parserOptions.project. Never disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any. Use strict: true in tsconfig. Run tsc --noEmit in CI.

evals.prompt: What tsconfig.json options should I use for a production TypeScript project?
evals.expected_output: Enable strict: true, noImplicitReturns: true, noUnusedLocals: true, noUnusedParameters: true, exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true. Set target and lib to match runtime. Use paths for module aliases. Run tsc --noEmit as part of CI.

evals.prompt: Should I use Jest or Vitest for testing a TypeScript project?
evals.expected_output: Prefer Vitest for new projects (faster, native ESM, better TypeScript support). Use Jest for projects with many existing test suites. Configure ts-jest or esbuild transformer for Jest. Aim for >80% coverage on critical paths.

should_not_trigger: How do I type a discriminated union in TypeScript?
How do I validate user input securely?
How do I write a React hook?
How do I organize React Native project structure?
